LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF FIRST REPUBLIC BANK'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION SHOULD NOT BE ISSUED AND RECEIVER BE APPOINTED (PER EX PARTE ORDER OF 01/15/10). ARGUED AND THE COURT VACATES ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULES AS FOLLOWS: THE HEARING IS CONTINUED FROM JAN-29-2010 TO FEB-19-2010, DEFENDANT'S OPPOSITION IS DUE 2-9-2010; PLAINTIFF'S REPLY IS DUE 2-16-1020, WITH COURTESY COPIES DELIVERED DIRECTLY TO THE COURT. E-MAIL TO DEFENDANT IS ALRIGHT WITH DEFENDANT IN PRO PER. IF DEFENDANT FINDS AN ATTORNEY TO REPRESENT HER, SHE MUST NOTIFY PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL. A TENTATIVE RULING WILL BE ISSUED BY THE COURT THE DAY BEFORE THE HEARING AT APPROXIMATELY 3:00 PM AND THE OTHER SIDE MUST BE CONTACTED BY 4:00 PM, IF EITHER SIDE WISHES TO ARGUE. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL WILL PREPARE ORDER WITH TODAY'S RULING FOR THE COURT'S SIGNATURE. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE CSR 5797

Abrams, Esq. of Greene Radovsky Maloney Share & Hennigh LLP (415) 981-1400 appearing for Plaintiff and Moving party; Catherine Kostlan, Defendant in Pro Per appearing and opposing party. Argued and the Court vacates its tentative ruling and rules as follows: The Hearing is continued to February 19, 2010, defendant's opposition is due 2-9-2010; plaintiff's reply is due 2-16-1020, with courtesy copies delivered directly to the Court. E-mail to Defendant is alright with Defendant in Pro Per. If defendant finds an attorney to represent her, she must notify Plaintiff's counsel. A tentative ruling will be issued by the Court the day before the hearing at approximately 3:00 pm and the other side must be contacted by 4:00 pm, if either side wishes to argue. Plaintiff's counsel will prepare Order with today's ruling for the Court's signature. (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
